title: Pauridia maximiliani
intro: Pauridia maximiliani, also known as the 'Cape starfish' or 'starflower', is a small, perennial plant species belonging to the family Amaryllidaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Pauridia maximiliani include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pauridia maximiliani is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a syrup or tincture, to leverage its health benefits.

This subfamily is further divided into several tribes, with Pauridia maximiliani being a part of the tribe Griffineae.
